issues.title: writeRecord helper core, per-record issue schema, and `issue create` CLI
issues.description: Stand up the single write path for issues end-to-end: author a per-record issue schema (one file = one issue, not the collection wrapper), widen the issue `id` pattern and the `prd` reference pattern (and the PRD schema's own `id`) to accept legacy `EXEC-NNN`/`PRD-NNN` OR UUIDv7, and build the `writeRecord` helper that mints a UUIDv7, rejects any caller-supplied id, derives a frozen slug from the title, validates against the per-record schema with a bundled ajv, serializes canonically, and writes atomically to `.excn/issues/<uuid>-<slug>.json`. Expose it as `to-execution issue create`. Demoable: create an issue from the CLI and get a schema-valid uuid-keyed file.
acceptance_criteria: `to-execution issue create --title ...` writes `.excn/issues/<uuid>-<slug>.json` with a valid UUIDv7 id and a slug derived (sanitized, lowercased) from the title
The helper rejects a record that arrives with an `id` field on create
A per-record issue schema exists and the helper validates against it; the legacy collection schema is retired or clearly scoped to archives
Issue `id` pattern, the `prd` reference pattern, and the PRD schema `id` pattern accept legacy ids OR UUIDv7
ajv is a declared package dependency and the validation path survives a fresh `npm install` (no host-root/pruned-module breakage) — sprint-6 lesson
All literals (uuid version, filename pattern, slug rules) are named constants, not inline — sprint-5 lesson
Serialization is library parse→mutate→stringify so malformed output (trailing comma) is structurally impossible — sprint-6 lesson
Write is temp-file + rename; a mid-write failure leaves no partial file
`.excn/.gitignore` does not catch `.excn/issues/*.json`; per-issue files stay git-tracked (ADR-0005, phantom-wipe class)
Helper unit tests cover: valid mint, supplied-id rejection, slug derivation, atomic write, schema-invalid rejection

issues.title: `issue update` and partition move through the helper
issues.description: Extend the helper and CLI with `to-execution issue update`: update any field except the immutable `id`, including status flips and moving an issue between partitions (backlog ↔ `sprint-<N>/`) by relocating its per-file record — location remains the lifecycle state. Reject any attempt to change the `id`.
acceptance_criteria: `to-execution issue update <id> --status ...` flips status and rewrites the file canonically
The `id` field is immutable; an update that tries to change it is rejected
Moving an issue into a sprint relocates its `<uuid>-<slug>.json` file to `.excn/issues/sprint-<N>/` (location-as-state preserved)
Other fields (assigned_sprint, notes, etc.) update without rewriting siblings
Update path reuses the helper's canonical serialization and atomic write

issues.title: Sprint authoring through the helper — whole-file write and step_log append
issues.description: Teach the helper to write sprint records (`kind=sprint`): whole-sprint create/update (scribe owns `sprint_<N>.json` at open/close) AND a `step_log` append mode (a gate adds one verdict without rewriting the file). Emit canonical-sentinel form — accreting arrays last, a constant sentinel key dead-last — so concurrent appends produce minimal, non-conflicting diffs. Expose `to-execution sprint ...`.
acceptance_criteria: `to-execution sprint` can create/update a whole sprint record and append a single `step_log` entry
Sprint files are emitted in canonical-sentinel form (arrays last, sentinel key dead-last) by a shared serializer
A single step_log append is a minimal byte-diff that does not touch sibling entries — sprint-4 step_log lesson
Whole-sprint authoring (open/close) goes through the helper, not just appends — H5
Sentinel-serializer constants are named, not inline — sprint-5 lesson

issues.title: Channel guard: deny raw writes to the issues and sprints homes (feature flag, default off)
issues.description: Add a PreToolUse channel guard that denies raw `Write`/`Edit` to `.excn/issues/` and `.excn/sprints/`, with a deny reason that redirects to the `to-execution` CLI. Model it on `progress-location-guard`: a `*-rules.cjs` data module (homes, deny-reason template, placeholder) separate from the decision logic. Add a `hooks-config` feature flag, default OFF in the Template (no auto-lockout); enabling on this Instance is sequenced later (EXEC-102, after CLI adoption).
acceptance_criteria: Raw `Write`/`Edit` to `.excn/issues/` or `.excn/sprints/` is denied with a reason that names the `to-execution` CLI
The guard never sees the helper's own writes (helper writes via fs, not the Write tool) — verified
Data/logic split mirrors `progress-location-guard`; homes and deny-reason are named constants in a `-rules.cjs` module — sprint-5 lesson
Fail-safe: missing/malformed config or payload, or internal error, exits 0 and never blocks a write (ADR-0006)
New `hooks-config` feature key added to the schema; default OFF in the Template
Guard subsumes the monolith-shape and uuid/serialization checks (no schema logic in the hook)

issues.title: Adopt the CLI in the team's own write-paths (clerk, scribe, gates)
issues.description: Move every live write-path the team uses for issues and sprints onto the `to-execution` CLI/helper: clerk's partition moves and status flips, scribe's sprint open/close, and the gates' step_log verdict appends. This must land before the channel guard is enabled on this Instance, or the running team locks itself out of recording its own work (H1). Agent-definition edits route through the Retro Loop / alignment gate.
acceptance_criteria: clerk, scribe, and the gate verdict-append path all write issues/sprints via the `to-execution` CLI, not by hand-editing JSON
No live team write-path depends on raw `Write`/`Edit` to `.excn/issues/` or `.excn/sprints/`
Agent-definition changes pass the alignment gate (Retro Loop)
Verified end-to-end: a full file→sprint→verdict cycle runs through the CLI with the guard enabled in a scratch check

issues.title: One-time `migrate-records` command: split all issue partitions, retrofit sprints, then enable the guard on the dogfood Instance
issues.description: Build a one-time `to-execution migrate-records` command (distinct from `migrate`, which stays location-only per ADR-0008): split `backlog.json` and every `sprint-<N>/sprint-<N>-issues.json` into per-file `<uuid>-<slug>.json` records (preserving — grandfathering — existing `EXEC-NNN` ids), and retrofit every `sprint_<N>.json` to canonical-sentinel form. All writes go through the helper (guard-safe). Then dogfood it on the root Instance on a clean tree and enable the channel-guard feature flag here.
acceptance_criteria: `migrate-records` splits `backlog.json` AND each sprint issue-companion file into per-file records, preserving legacy ids
Every `sprint_<N>.json` is retrofitted to canonical-sentinel form via the helper
Repo root is resolved by absolute path, never an inherited cwd — sprint-4 postmortem
Every written record re-reads schema-valid BEFORE any monolith is deleted — verify-before-destroy, postmortem
Idempotent: a second run is a no-op; it never clobbers an existing per-file record — sprint-6 restamp lesson
Inputs are committed first; recovery is a documented `git checkout` — postmortem
Dogfood run is sequenced by the Team Lead on a clean tree with no WIP lanes; verification Bash uses no `&&` chain that could trigger a file-reverting linter — sprint-8 lesson
After migration completes and CLI adoption (EXEC-101) is in place, the channel-guard flag is enabled on this Instance

issues.title: Stamp the per-file layout into `src/template` (fresh-Instance path)
issues.description: Src-First: land the new per-record schema, the `to-execution issue/sprint` CLI, the channel-guard hook + its `hooks-config` flag, and the empty `.excn/issues/` layout in `src/template` so a freshly-stamped Instance starts in the per-file world with no `backlog.json` ever. The fresh path must not error on an empty issues directory.
acceptance_criteria: A fresh `npx to-execution init` stamps the per-record schema, the issue/sprint CLI, and the channel-guard hook + flag
A fresh Instance has an empty `.excn/issues/` and no `backlog.json`; nothing errors on the empty directory
The stamped `.gitignore` keeps `.excn/issues/*.json` tracked (not caught by the `*_progress.json` ignore) — phantom-wipe class
Changes land in `src/` first; the root Instance mirrors afterward (Src-First)
package-qa and code-standards gates pass on the stamped output

issues.title: Viewer reads the per-file issues directory
issues.description: Point the status-page viewer at the per-file layout: glob `.excn/issues/**/*.json` instead of reading `backlog.json`. Handle an empty issues directory and a missing `backlog.json` (fresh-Instance path) without erroring, and sort sanely when legacy ids (no uuid timestamp prefix) and UUIDv7 ids coexist.
acceptance_criteria: The viewer renders issues by globbing `.excn/issues/**/*.json`, not by reading `backlog.json`
An empty `.excn/issues/` and a missing `backlog.json` render cleanly (fresh-Instance path) — G7
Mixed legacy-id and UUIDv7 records sort in a sane, stable order; the uuid-prefix=creation-order claim degrades gracefully for legacy ids
code-standards gate passes

issues.title: `execution-to-prd` and the grill mint UUIDv7 for new PRDs and ADRs
issues.description: Update the `execution-to-prd` skill and the grill so newly authored PRDs (JSON) and ADRs (markdown) mint a UUIDv7 id instead of the next sequential number. Must ship only after the `prd`/PRD id patterns are widened (EXEC-097), or references to a UUID PRD fail validation (G5).
acceptance_criteria: A new PRD authored by `execution-to-prd` carries a UUIDv7 id and a slug-suffixed filename
A new ADR carries a UUIDv7 in its frontmatter
Does not ship before EXEC-097's pattern widening; a UUID PRD validates and is referenceable by issues
Skill edits read fresh from disk (no stale-content / linter-revert window) — sprint-8 lesson
